channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Majority of Filipinos Express Trust in PNP, OCTA Survey Shows
item.description: A recent survey conducted by OCTA Research from March 19 to 25, 2026, found that 63 percent of Filipinos either strongly or somewhat trust the Philippine National Police (PNP). The survey also reveale...

item.title: Ted Turner, CNN Founder and Cable TV Innovator, Passes Away at 87
item.description: Ted Turner, the visionary media mogul and founder of CNN, has passed away at the age of 87. Widely recognized for revolutionizing the television industry, Turner transformed news broadcasting by intro...

item.title: Monica Juma Appointed to Lead Two Key UN Offices After Serving as Ruto’s Security Advisor
item.description: President William Ruto has officially seen off Monica Juma as she prepares to assume her new roles at the United Nations in Vienna, Austria. Juma, who served as Ruto’s National Security Advisor for th...

item.title: New York Fed Study Shows Rising Gas Prices Hurt Lower-Income Households More
item.description: A new analysis from the Federal Reserve Bank of New York says that the recent jump in gasoline prices has affected lower-income households much more severely than higher-income households. The report ...

item.title: DA Introduces Economic Inclusion for All Bill in Parliament to Reform BEE Policy
item.description: The Democratic Alliance (DA) has formally introduced the Economic Inclusion for All Bill in Parliament, seeking to replace the African National Congress's (ANC) Black Economic Empowerment (BEE) policy...

item.title: Detained Nigerian Soldier Dies After Alleged Medical Neglect in Army Custody, Another Faces Serious Health Issues
item.description: The Nigerian Army dey face serious questions following the death of Corporal Bala Hudu, wey don dey detained since October 2023 for alleged self-defence. According to sources, Hudu sick on April 4, 20...

item.title: NASA Satellite Data Shows Uneven Subsidence Rates Across Mexico City
item.description: Mexico City is experiencing significant land subsidence, and new data from NASA’s NISAR satellite provides a clearer picture of how quickly and unevenly the ground is sinking. The satellite, developed...

item.title: Borno State Budget Allocates ₦900m for Government House Furniture Amid Security and Humanitarian Challenges
item.description: Borno State dey cause gbege after dem allocate around ₦900 million for furniture inside the 2026 'Budget of Sustained Recovery and Growth.' This allocation come for government house, even as state sti...

item.title: Bonnie Tyler recovering after emergency intestinal surgery in Portugal
item.description: Welsh singer Bonnie Tyler, best known for her hit “Total Eclipse of the Heart”, has undergone emergency bowel surgery after being admitted to hospital in Faro, Portugal. The 74-year-old artist, who ow...

item.title: Michael Rapaport Criticizes NY Leadership After Anti-Israel Protest Outside Manhattan Synagogue
item.description: Comedian Michael Rapaport publicly condemned New York leadership following a protest outside Park East Synagogue in Manhattan, where around 100 demonstrators gathered. Anti-Israel protesters, some wav...

item.title: KMTC Alerts Students on Fraudulent Letter Promising Admission and Course Changes
item.description: The Kenya Medical Training College (KMTC) has warned students about a fake letter circulating online that falsely promises help with admission letters, course changes, transfers, and deferments. The l...

item.title: YSR Congress Party urges careful monitoring of electoral roll revision
item.description: The YSR Congress Party (YSRCP) has called for heightened vigilance during the ongoing Special Intensive Revision (SIR) of electoral rolls in Andhra Pradesh. Sajjala Ramakrishna Reddy, the party's Stat...

item.title: Russian Medical Universities to Participate in Education Fair Across Tamil Nadu
item.description: This month, Tamil Nadu will host an All India Russian Education Fair, featuring participation from 18 top medical universities of the Russian Federation. Organisers have announced that the fair will p...

item.title: Long-Term Study Finds Partial Meniscectomy Offers No Benefit and May Harm Patients
item.description: A 10-year clinical trial led by the University of Helsinki has challenged the effectiveness of partial meniscectomy, a widely performed knee surgery intended to relieve pain from meniscus tears. The F...

item.title: Ex-JPMorgan Banker Pursues Legal Case Against Former Boss Over Alleged Workplace Misconduct
item.description: Chirayu Rana, a 35-year-old former JPMorgan Chase banker, has initiated legal action against his ex-boss, Lorna Hajdini, claiming she subjected him to sexual and racial abuse while using her position ...

item.title: U.S. prosecutors accuse man of using the Astor family name in a $450 million fraud scheme
item.description: U.S. prosecutors have accused a man with multiple aliases of using the prestige of the Astor family name to carry out a large fraud against Mexican billionaire Ricardo Salinas Pliego. According to a n...

item.title: Chalmers signals modest deficit reduction in upcoming budget despite RBA concerns
item.description: Treasurer Jim Chalmers is set to announce a modest improvement in Australia's budget deficit in next week's federal budget, aiming to counter criticism that Labor’s spending has contributed to higher ...

item.title: Practical ways to reduce heat loss and save on energy bills this winter
item.description: As energy bills rise and winter approaches, many Australians are seeking affordable ways to keep their homes warm. While conventional advice often focuses on heaters and insulation, significant energy...

item.title: Two Low-Intensity Explosions Occur Near Military Sites in Punjab, No Casualties Reported
item.description: On Tuesday night, Punjab witnessed two separate low-intensity explosions close to military installations in Jalandhar and Amritsar. The first incident occurred around 8 pm in Jalandhar when a scooter ...

item.title: David Mark Acknowledge Obi and Kwankwaso Defection Affect ADC, Pledges Stronger Efforts
item.description: The National Chairman of the African Democratic Congress (ADC), Senator David Mark, don openly admit say the recent exit of former presidential candidates, Peter Obi and Rabiu Kwankwaso, don affect th...
